traits.quirk: has an enemy (Brakkun, a dwarf disgraced soldier, level 3, out there somewhere)

q01.name: The Rune-Fire Below
q01.desc: Chanters feeding forbidden runes in a sealed gallery. The runesmiths want the fire out before the stone learns it.
q01.epilogue: The vault is re-sealed under three locks and a new rune; the warden files the party's names under 'safe hands'.

q02.name: The Grudge War
q02.desc: A rival clan pressed an old grudge with new axes. The ledger wants balancing.
q02.epilogue: The ledger line is struck through in both clans' books -- balanced, the keeper says, for a generation at least.

q03.name: Things in the Deep Roads
q03.desc: The under-way to the second hold is silked and skittering. The toll-guild pays by the cleared mile.
q03.epilogue: The under-way reopens by the cleared mile, tolls half-price for a season in the party's honor.

q04.name: The Flooded Hold
q04.desc: An old hold, lost and lately dry again. What was buried with it did not stay buried.
q04.epilogue: The heir walks the dry galleries once, dry-eyed, and seals the hold behind -- some doors are better shut.

q05.name: The Renegade Magus
q05.desc: A court wizard who stopped asking leave. The crown pays for the tower dark and the fires out.
q05.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q06.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q06.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q06.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q07.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q07.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q07.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q08.name: The Boiler-Cult
q08.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q08.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Tizzy, age 8)

q09.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q09.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q09.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q10.name: The Coven in the Mists
q10.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q10.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q11.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q11.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q11.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q12.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q12.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q12.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q13.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q13.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q13.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q14.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q14.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q14.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q15.name: Rival Warband
q15.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q15.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q16.name: The Proving Hunt
q16.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q16.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q17.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q17.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q17.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q18.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q18.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q18.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q19.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q19.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q19.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q20.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q20.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q20.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q21.name: The Restless Crypt
q21.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q21.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q22.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q22.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q22.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q23.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q23.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q23.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q24.name: Wolves at the Folds
q24.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q24.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q25.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q25.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q25.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q26.name: Webs in the Canopy
q26.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q26.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Berrian
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q27.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q27.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q27.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q28.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q28.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q28.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Vexa, age 11)

q29.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q29.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q29.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q30.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q30.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q30.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q31.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q31.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q31.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q32.name: The Breaker in the Dark
q32.desc: Something big moved into the worked seams and it does not share. The mine masters want their tunnels back.
q32.epilogue: The seams ring with picks again by week's end.

q33.name: The Blighted Glade
q33.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.
q33.epilogue: Green returns to the glade's edge within the season; what soured the heartwood is burned and sung over.

q34.name: The Giant of the Border Marches
q34.desc: Border forts flattened, garrisons walking home weaponless. The marshal wants the marches quiet again.
q34.epilogue: The forts are re-garrisoned; the marches go quiet, and stay so.

q35.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q35.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q35.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q36.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q36.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q36.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

q37.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q37.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q37.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q38.name: The Boiler-Cult
q38.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q38.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q43.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q44.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q44.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q44.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q45.name: Medicine for the Outbreak
q45.desc: A sickness across the border, and the cure sitting here in a stockroom. Every day on the road is counted in graves.
q45.epilogue: The crate is empty within two days and the burial details stand down; the warden keeps the party's names for the record of the outbreak.

q46.name: The Sealed Dispatch
q46.desc: Court business under wax, and a rider who never arrived. Someone would rather it never does.
q46.epilogue: The seal is broken behind a shut door; whatever it says, couriers ride the same road twice as guarded within the week.
